reference_answer: The standard payment term is Net 30 days from the invoice issue date.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention Net 30 days as the standard payment term.

reference_answer: A grace period of up to 5 calendar days after the invoice due date is applied before late penalties are enforced.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention 5 calendar days as the grace period.

reference_answer: A late fee of 2% of the outstanding invoice amount applies after the grace period.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention 2% late fee on outstanding invoice amount.

reference_answer: Purchase orders above 25,000 EUR require director-level approval.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention director-level approval for orders above 25,000 EUR.

reference_answer: Vendors must submit: a completed vendor registration form, tax identification documents, verified bank account details, and compliance certifications such as ISO or regulatory licenses.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention at least 3 of: registration form, tax ID, bank account details, compliance certifications.

reference_answer: Invoices failing the matching process are flagged for review by the accounts payable team. Disputed invoices may be returned to the vendor for correction. The ERP system prevents payment until resolved.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention flagging for review and blocking payment until resolved.

reference_answer: Suppliers may offer early payment discounts expressed as 2/10 Net 30, meaning a 2% discount is available if payment is made within 10 days.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention 2% discount available within 10 days (2/10 Net 30).

reference_answer: A price variance tolerance of up to 2% is allowed between the invoice and the purchase order. Quantity variances may be tolerated up to 5%.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention 2% price variance tolerance.

reference_answer: Purchase orders between 5,000 EUR and 25,000 EUR require procurement manager approval.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention procurement manager approval for orders between 5,000 and 25,000 EUR.

reference_answer: Vendor onboarding follows a three-stage approval workflow: procurement review, finance validation, and compliance verification. The ERP system automatically tracks approval status and audit logs.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention all three stages: procurement review, finance validation, compliance verification.

reference_answer: Compliance checks during vendor onboarding include verification of compliance certifications (ISO or regulatory licenses), anti-corruption policy adherence, and conflict of interest disclosure.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention compliance certifications and regulatory requirements.

reference_answer: Vendors are assigned a credit rating or risk category. High-risk vendors may require additional approval from senior procurement leadership.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention credit rating/risk category assignment and senior approval for high-risk vendors.

reference_answer: The procurement governance policy outlines procurement governance, supplier selection procedures, and compliance expectations within the ERP system.
scoring_criteria: Answer must describe the governance purpose around supplier selection and compliance.

reference_answer: The purpose of the invoice matching process is to ensure that invoices correspond to approved procurement transactions by validating them against purchase orders and goods receipts.
scoring_criteria: Answer must explain matching ensures invoices correspond to approved transactions.

reference_answer: The ERP system automatically identifies eligible early payment discounts during invoice processing. Discounts such as 2/10 Net 30 are recognized and applied automatically.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention the ERP automatically identifies and applies early payment discounts.

reference_answer: Invoices exceeding the allowed price tolerance are flagged by the ERP system and payment is prevented until the mismatch is resolved.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention flagging and payment prevention when tolerance is exceeded.

reference_answer: The vendor master record stores: vendor registration details, tax identification, bank account details, compliance certifications, credit rating or risk category, and any custom payment term agreements.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention at least 4 types of vendor master data.

reference_answer: The main steps of the purchase order lifecycle are: creation, approval, supplier confirmation, delivery tracking, and invoice matching.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention at least 4 of the 5 lifecycle steps.

reference_answer: High-value purchases require competitive supplier quotations and procurement must verify budget availability before issuing the purchase order.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention competitive quotations and budget verification for high-value purchases.

reference_answer: The procurement department evaluates suppliers based on cost, quality, delivery reliability, and compliance certifications. Competitive bidding is required for high-value procurements.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention evaluation criteria including cost, quality, delivery, and competitive bidding.

reference_answer: Standard payment term is Net 30 days. A 5-day grace period applies before a 2% late fee is charged. Early payment discount of 2/10 Net 30 is available. ERP enforces schedules automatically. Strategic vendors may have custom terms (Net 45/60) recorded in vendor master.
scoring_criteria: Answer must cover payment terms, grace period, late fee, and early payment discount comprehensively.

reference_answer: Three-way matching validates invoices against purchase orders, goods receipts, and supplier invoices. Price variance up to 2% and quantity variance up to 5% are tolerated. Mismatches beyond tolerance are flagged and payment is blocked until resolved.
scoring_criteria: Answer must explain all three documents in the match, tolerance percentages, and what happens on failure.

reference_answer: Vendor onboarding follows three stages: procurement review, finance validation, compliance verification. Required documents include registration form, tax ID, bank account details, compliance certifications. High-risk vendors need senior approval. ERP tracks approval status automatically.
scoring_criteria: Answer must describe all three approval stages and documentation requirements.

reference_answer: Procurement ensures supplier compliance through: mandatory compliance certifications, anti-corruption policy adherence, conflict of interest disclosures, competitive bidding for high-value purchases, and ongoing risk assessment.
scoring_criteria: Answer must cover at least 3 supplier compliance mechanisms.

reference_answer: Invoice disputes arise when matching fails tolerance checks. The accounts payable team reviews flagged invoices. Disputed invoices may be returned to the vendor for correction. The ERP prevents payment until the dispute is resolved.
scoring_criteria: Answer must explain the dispute process: flagging, AP review, vendor return, resolution before payment.

reference_answer: POs below 5,000 EUR: department manager approval. POs 5,000–25,000 EUR: procurement manager approval. POs above 25,000 EUR: director-level approval. High-value POs require competitive quotations and budget verification. All POs are audit-logged.
scoring_criteria: Answer must cover all three approval tiers with correct EUR thresholds.

reference_answer: Controls include three-way matching, tolerance thresholds (2% price, 5% quantity), automatic flagging of mismatches, payment blocking until resolved, and audit logging of all transactions.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention matching process and at least 3 specific controls.

reference_answer: Financial controls include: purchase order approval thresholds, invoice matching with tolerance rules, early payment discount management, late fee enforcement, dual approval for vendor bank detail changes, and full audit logging.
scoring_criteria: Answer must describe at least 4 distinct financial controls.

reference_answer: Supplier selection is based on cost, quality, delivery reliability, and compliance certifications. Competitive bidding is required for high-value purchases. RFQs must be issued to multiple suppliers. Long-term agreements are documented in the ERP contract management module.
scoring_criteria: Answer must describe evaluation criteria, competitive bidding, and RFQ process.

reference_answer: All purchase orders are logged in the ERP system and subject to internal audit review. Approval at each threshold is recorded. High-value POs require competitive quotations as documented evidence.
scoring_criteria: Answer must mention audit logging and internal review of purchase orders.

reference_answer: A list of top 5 customers ranked by total order value with customer names and revenue figures.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return rows with customer names and order values, sorted descending, 5 or fewer rows.

reference_answer: A ranked list of top 10 products by quantity sold.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return product names and quantities sold.

reference_answer: A list of the 10 most recent orders with order IDs and timestamps.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return recent orders with order IDs.

scoring_criteria: Answer must return customer names and total payment amounts ranked.

reference_answer: A list of products sorted by price descending.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return product names and prices.

reference_answer: A list of recent sales orders from the Odoo sales orders table.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return sales order references and creation dates.

reference_answer: Total sales amount grouped by country.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return country names and corresponding total sales amounts.

reference_answer: Product categories ranked by total revenue generated.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return category names and revenue totals.

reference_answer: Customers ranked by number of orders placed.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return customer names and order counts.

reference_answer: A single numeric value representing the average order value across all orders.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return an average order value as a numeric result.

reference_answer: Average order value grouped by device type (desktop, mobile, tablet).
scoring_criteria: Answer must return device types and average order values per device.

reference_answer: Payment methods ranked by frequency of use.

reference_answer: Products ranked by profit margin (margin_usd).
scoring_criteria: Answer must return product names and margin values.

reference_answer: Customers sorted by signup_date descending showing most recently signed up.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return customer names and signup dates in recency order.

scoring_criteria: Answer must return product names and revenue totals.

scoring_criteria: Answer must return country names and total sales figures.

reference_answer: Customers ranked by total amount spent (sum of order totals).
scoring_criteria: Answer must return customer names and total spend figures.

reference_answer: Products ranked by frequency of appearance in orders (total quantity sold).
scoring_criteria: Answer must return product names and frequency/quantity counts.

reference_answer: Largest orders by total_usd value with customer information.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return order IDs or customer names and large order values.

reference_answer: Count of orders per customer.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return customer identifiers and order counts.

reference_answer: Average margin_usd grouped by product category.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return category names and average margin values.

reference_answer: Products ranked by total profit (sum of margin_usd across all order_items).
scoring_criteria: Answer must return product names and total profit figures.

reference_answer: Average order value per country.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return country names and average order values.

reference_answer: Customers ranked by lifetime value (sum of total_usd across all their orders).
scoring_criteria: Answer must return customer names and lifetime value totals.

reference_answer: Product pairs that appear together most often in the same order, using a self-join on order_items.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return product name pairs and co-purchase counts.

reference_answer: Distribution of orders by device type (count per device type).
scoring_criteria: Answer must return device types and order counts.

reference_answer: Product categories ranked by total revenue.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return categories and revenue totals.

reference_answer: Customers ranked by count of high-value orders (above average order value).
scoring_criteria: Answer must return customer names and high-value order counts.

reference_answer: Total revenue generated from each product.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return product names and total revenue figures.

reference_answer: Revenue contribution percentage or total per product category.
scoring_criteria: Answer must return category names and revenue contribution.

reference_answer: Document part: Three-way matching validates invoices against purchase orders, goods receipts, and supplier invoices. Tolerances: 2% price, 5% quantity. DB part: Product categories ranked by average margin.
scoring_criteria: Answer must describe the three-way match process AND show category margin data. Complex composite query likely to trigger corrective retry.

reference_answer: Document part: Procurement governance covers supplier selection, competitive bidding, compliance. DB part: Total purchase order values grouped by vendor from purchase_orders_odoo.
